78.reason: [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it can cause buffer overflow. However, the immediate error is that the merging logic only merges adjacent duplicates once, missing cases where multiple same exponents exist non-consecutively after sorting. Also, the merging loop condition `while(current->next!=NULL)` skips merging the last node if it matches the previous one.]

79.reason: [Same issue as above with `gets`. Additionally, the program does not handle zero coefficients after merging 鈥 terms with zero coefficient should be removed from the output.]

145.reason: [This loop only checks consecutive pairs and stops before the last node. If the last two nodes have the same exponent, they won't be merged. Also, after merging, the new coefficient might become zero, which should be removed, but isn't handled. Moreover, if multiple terms share the same exponent (more than two), this single-pass merge will miss some merges because it doesn't re-check after deletion.]
